content.problemStatement:
You are given two integers aaa and bbb.

The score of an array AAA of length NNN, denoted S(A)S(A)S(A), is defined to be

S(A)=a⋅(∑i=1N∑j=i+1N∣Ai−Aj∣)+b⋅∑i=1NAiS(A) = a\cdot \left(\sum_{i=1}^N \sum_{j=i+1}^N |A_i - A_j|\right) + b\cdot \sum_{i=1}^N A_iS(A)=a⋅(i=1∑N​j=i+1∑N​∣Ai​−Aj​∣)+b⋅i=1∑N​Ai​You are also given an integer KKK.

Find the minimum possible length of a non-empty array AAA for which S(A)=KS(A) = KS(A)=K, or decide that no such array exists.

If a valid array exists, you should also find any valid array of minimum length.

The array you construct should satisfy −1018≤Ai≤1018-10^{18} \leq A_i \leq 10^{18}−1018≤Ai​≤1018 for every iii.

It can be shown that for the given constraints, if a valid array exists, then one satisfying the above constraint on elements exists as well.

**The input is generated in such a way that the sum of sizes of all output arrays will not exceed 10610^6106.**


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case contains three space-separated integers a,b,a, b, a,b, and KKK - the parameters mentioned in the statement.


Output Format
For each test case,



If no valid array exists, print a single line containing the integer −1-1−1.
Otherwise, print two lines.

The first line should contain a single integer NNN, the size of the smallest array having score exactly equal to KKK.
The second line should contain NNN space-separated integers denoting the elements of the array.

Each integer printed on this line should be between −1018-10^{18}−1018 and 101810^{18}1018, inclusive.



If there are multiple solutions, you may print any of them.
constraints: The input is generated in such a way that the sum of sizes of all output arrays will not exceed 10610^6106.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Since a=b=0a = b = 0a=b=0, any array has a score of 000.
So, the minimum valid array length is 111, and any array of length 111 with its element being between −1018-10^{18}−1018 and 101810^{18}1018 will be accepted.
Test case 222: With a=2a = 2a=2 and b=6b = 6b=6, it can be proved that there exists no array of size 111 having score exactly equal to 525252.
The array A=[7,−1]A = [7, -1]A=[7,−1] is one of the possible solutions, having S(A)=2⋅∣7−(−1)∣+6⋅(7+(−1))=52S(A) = 2 \cdot |7 - (-1)| + 6 \cdot (7 + (-1)) = 52S(A)=2⋅∣7−(−1)∣+6⋅(7+(−1))=52
Test case 333: With a=0a = 0a=0 and b=11b = 11b=11, it can be proved that there exists no array having a score exactly equal to 181818.
